perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party " means the party receiving Confidential Information, including any individual or indi viduals and "the recipient" mea ns the party receiving it in the particular case.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Entity " means the party receiving Confidential Information, including any individual or indi viduals and "the recipient" mea ns the party receiving it in the particular case.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Receiving Entity' creates uncertainty. The contract uses 'Receiving Party' throughout, so this redefinition raises questions about whether the obligations apply to the 'Receiving Entity' or the 'Receiving Party', if they are distinct. The redefinition created confusion, since the 'Receiving Party' is used and referenced in multiple places in the text. This contradiction makes enforcement difficult.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with the Business Purpose it will be necessary fo r each party, either itself or through a third party acting as agent for it, to disclose to the other party Confidential Informa tion of the disclosing party, w h i c h m a y b e communicated orally, in document form, by demonstration or othe rwise.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Receiving Party' to 'Receiving Entity' introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to each 'party' disclosing information to the other 'party'. By redefining 'Receiving Party' as 'Receiving Entity', it creates ambiguity about which entity the disclosing party is sharing information with, especially given the widespread use of 'Receiving Party' throughout the document. This misalignment of terminology, as described in the explanation and the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, directly impacts the clarity and enforceability of the original disclosure obligation, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In connection with the Business Purpose it will be necessary fo r each party, either itself or through a third party acting as agent for it, to disclose to the other party Confidential Informa tion of the disclosing party, w h i c h m a y b e communicated orally, in document form, by demonstration or othe rwise.
perturbation.changed_text: In connection with the Project it will be necessary fo r each party, either itself or through a third party acting as agent for it, to disclose to the other party Confidential Informa tion of the disclosing party, w h i c h m a y b e communicated orally, in document form, by demonstration or othe rwise.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Business Purpose' with 'Project' in this section, while other sections still refer to 'Business Purpose', creates a contradiction. It's now unclear if 'Project' and 'Business Purpose' are the same, affecting the scope of the NDA and the permitted use of the information. Since most of the text references the 'Business Purpose', the text above is creating an in-text contradition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: not to use or circulate such Confidential Information withi n its own organisation except solely to the extent necessary for the purp oses of the Business Purpose or any other purpose the disclosing party may hereafter expressly authorise in writing;
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Business Purpose' to 'Project' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Business Purpose' as the permitted use of confidential information. By changing 'Business Purpose' to 'Project' in the definition, it is unclear whether 'Project' and 'Business Purpose' are the same, thus contradicting the limitation of use outlined in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, where using multiple terms interchangeably without clear definition leads to conflicting obligations. The change introduces ambiguity and potential unenforceability, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party undertakes in respect of Confidential Information fo r which it is the recipient:‐ ሺaሻ to treat such Confidential Information disclosed by the dis closing party as confidential; ሺbሻ not without the disclosing party's prior written consent in each case to communicate or disclose any part of such Confidential Informati on to any person except:‐ ሺiሻ only to those personnel of t he recipient on a need to know basis who are concerned with the Business Purpose; ሺiiሻ the recipient's auditors and professional advisers and any other persons or bodies having a legal right or duty to have a c c e s s t o o r k n o w l e d g e o f t h e C o n f i d e n t i a l I n f o r m a t i o n i n connection with the business of the recipient; ሺiiiሻ where the recipient is ordered by a court of competent jurisdiction to do so or there is a statutory obligation to do so except that the recipient shall use all reasonable endeavours t o f i r s t i n f o r m t h e d i s c l o s i n g p a r t y i n w r i t i n g b e f o r e a n y disclosure under such order or obligation is made; and ሺivሻ to third parties engaged by the recipient who are concerne d with the Business Purpose and who have been expressly authorised in writing by the disclosing party to receive the Confidential Information prior to disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party undertakes in respect of Sensitive Information fo r which it is the recipient:‐ ሺaሻ to treat such Sensitive Information disclosed by the dis closing party as confidential; ሺbሻ not without the disclosing party's prior written consent in each case to communicate or disclose any part of such Confidential Informati on to any person except:‐ ሺiሻ only to those personnel of t he recipient on a need to know basis who are concerned with the Business Purpose; ሺiiሻ the recipient's auditors and professional advisers and any other persons or bodies having a legal right or duty to have a c c e s s t o o r k n o w l e d g e o f t h e C o n f i d e n t i a l I n f o r m a t i o n i n connection with the business of the recipient; ሺiiiሻ where the recipient is ordered by a court of competent jurisdiction to do so or there is a statutory obligation to do so except that the recipient shall use all reasonable endeavours t o f i r s t i n f o r m t h e d i s c l o s i n g p a r t y i n w r i t i n g b e f o r e a n y disclosure under such order or obligation is made; and ሺivሻ to third parties engaged by the recipient who are concerne d with the Business Purpose and who have been expressly authorised in writing by the disclosing party to receive the Confidential Information prior to disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' in subsection (a), while subsection (b) and subsequent parts of the clause still refer to 'Confidential Information', creates an in-text contradiction within the same clause. This creates ambiguity as to which type of information each part of the clause refers to, undermining the obligations of confidentiality. Since subsection (b) and subsequent parts still refer to 'Confidential Information', this creates an in-text contradition to which type of information each part of the clause refers to, undermining the obligations of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party undertakes in respect of Sensitive Information fo r which it is the recipient:‐ ሺaሻ to treat such Sensitive Information disclosed by the dis closing party as confidential; ሺbሻ not without the disclosing party's prior written consent in each case to communicate or disclose any part of such Confidential Informati on to any person except:‐ ሺiሻ only to those personnel of t he recipient on a need to know basis who are concerned with the Business Purpose; ሺiiሻ the recipient's auditors and professional advisers and any other persons or bodies having a legal right or duty to have a c c e s s t o o r k n o w l e d g e o f t h e C o n f i d e n t i a l I n f o r m a t i o n i n connection with the business of the recipient; ሺiiiሻ where the recipient is ordered by a court of competent jurisdiction to do so or there is a statutory obligation to do so except that the recipient shall use all reasonable endeavours t o f i r s t i n f o r m t h e d i s c l o s i n g p a r t y i n w r i t i n g b e f o r e a n y disclosure under such order or obligation is made; and ሺivሻ to third parties engaged by the recipient who are concerne d with the Business Purpose and who have been expressly authorised in writing by the disclosing party to receive the Confidential Information prior to disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' in subsection (a) of the clause. However, subsection (b) and subsequent parts of the same clause still refer to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct conflict within the clause, because it refers to two different types of information, while supposedly being about the same undertaking. The 'Contradicted Text' is the changed text itself, since the in-text contradiction appears in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the completion or termination of the Business Purpose, eac h recipient shall promptly deliver up to the disclosing party all Materials supplied by the disclosing party incorporating any Confidential Information of that party and all copies thereof and destroy or erase any Confidential Informatio n contained in any materials and documentation prepared by or on behalf of the recipient or recorded in any memory device. Within fourteen ሺ14ሻ days of suc h request or completion of the Business Purpose the recipient shall certify in writing to the disclosing party that it has fully complied with its obligation s under this Clause. Notwithstanding the foregoing each recipient may retain one cop y of all Materials containing Confidential Information of the disclosing party received or made in connection with this Agreement for archival purposes only, subject always to strict compliance with the obligations of Clauses 3 and 5.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the completion or termination of the Project, eac h recipient shall promptly deliver up to the disclosing party all Materials supplied by the disclosing party incorporating any Confidential Information of that party and all copies thereof and destroy or erase any Confidential Informatio n contained in any materials and documentation prepared by or on behalf of the recipient or recorded in any memory device. Within fourteen ሺ14ሻ days of suc h request or completion of the Assignment the recipient shall certify in writing to the disclosing party that it has fully complied with its obligation s under this Clause. Notwithstanding the foregoing each recipient may retain one cop y of all Materials containing Confidential Information of the disclosing party received or made in connection with this Agreement for archival purposes only, subject always to strict compliance with the obligations of Clauses 3 and 5.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Business Purpose' with 'Project' and 'Assignment' in different instances within the same paragraph regarding termination obligations introduces significant ambiguity. It's now unclear what event triggers the return of materials, creating a direct contradiction. 'Project', 'Business Purpose' and 'Assignment' are undefined separately, so their interchangeability creates confusion. This will create in-text contradition to the scope and term. Because different instances of those are present in the paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the completion or termination of the Project, eac h recipient shall promptly deliver up to the disclosing party all Materials supplied by the disclosing party incorporating any Confidential Information of that party and all copies thereof and destroy or erase any Confidential Informatio n contained in any materials and documentation prepared by or on behalf of the recipient or recorded in any memory device. Within fourteen ሺ14ሻ days of suc h request or completion of the Assignment the recipient shall certify in writing to the disclosing party that it has fully complied with its obligation s under this Clause. Notwithstanding the foregoing each recipient may retain one cop y of all Materials containing Confidential Information of the disclosing party received or made in connection with this Agreement for archival purposes only, subject always to strict compliance with the obligations of Clauses 3 and 5.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Business Purpose' with 'Project' in one instance and 'Assignment' in another within the same paragraph. The 'Project', 'Business Purpose' and 'Assignment' are undefined separately, so their interchangeability creates confusion regarding the trigger for returning materials, leading to a conflict with itself, which is repeated verbatim in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
